text.title: SEO & AEO Mastery Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every SEO & AEO Mastery application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Optimise every indexable page for BOTH traditional search engines (Google, Bing) AND AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude) simultaneously: clear semantic structure, authoritative source citations, parseable schema markup, fast loading. Single-path optimisation forfeits the other channel.
rule.rationale: The same technical quality that satisfies Google also enables AI crawlers to cite the content; treating SEO and AEO as separate budgets duplicates effort with no compounding return.

rule.statement: Surface Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness signals explicitly: named author with bio + credentials, last-reviewed date, citations to authoritative sources, HTTPS, contact + privacy + about pages. Missing any one — particularly Trustworthiness — caps ranking potential regardless of content quality.
rule.rationale: Google uses E-E-A-T as a meta-quality evaluator; LLMs use the same surface signals to decide whether to cite the source.

rule.statement: Publish a `/llms.txt` at the domain root naming canonical pages and excluded paths; structure article bodies for AEO (TL;DR up-top, definition + answer-first paragraph, scannable headings, named entities consistent site-wide).
rule.rationale: AI crawlers lack the traditional crawler context; llms.txt + AEO-shaped bodies reduce hallucination + improve citation accuracy.

rule.statement: For multilingual or multi-regional sites, ship hreflang annotations on every alternate URL with bidirectional self-reference and `x-default` for the fallback; validate via the hreflang report in GSC. Missing or asymmetric hreflang causes Google to pick the wrong locale for the wrong audience.
rule.rationale: Asymmetric hreflang is one of the most common multilingual SEO failures and routes EU readers to US pages (or worse).

rule.statement: Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) MUST pass the green threshold in CrUX before content or link investment scales; failing CWV gates the rest. Audit monthly; a regression in any metric >20% week-over-week triggers an investigation before new publishing resumes.
rule.rationale: CWV is a confirmed ranking factor; CWV regression silently demotes the whole property and masks otherwise-working SEO work.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
